questions: Identify the demographic estimation resource that describes itself as the major output of a joint IUSSP and UNFPA project and a direct line of descent from 'UN Manual X'. Within this resource, locate the 2011 section authored by T.A. Moultrie regarding the evaluation of fertility data. Provide the exact title of this section and the full list of editors for the parent volume as they appear in the suggested citation.

Identify the major digital resource developed jointly by the IUSSP and UNFPA that serves as a direct successor to the United Nations Manual X, focused on demographic estimation from limited or defective data. Once this resource is located, provide a technical and functional overview by answering the following: Which specific web browsers are explicitly recommended for the best viewing experience? What is the critical content difference between the downloadable 2013 PDF version of the material and the live website? Finally, distinguish which user capabilities are freely available versus those that strictly require user registration.

Identify the comprehensive digital resource for demographic estimation that was produced as a joint project between the IUSSP and UNFPA, serving as the direct lineage successor to the UN Manual X: Indirect Techniques for Demographic Estimation. Within this resource, locate the specific chapter or section authored by T.A. Moultrie that addresses the 'evaluation and correction of fertility data.' Provide the full suggested citation for this specific section, ensuring you include the author, the year, the chapter title, the full list of editors for the parent volume, and the publisher.

rounds.rationale: The question masks the name of the website/book ('Tools for Demographic Estimation') behind its historical context and organizational origins (Deep Logic). rounds.meta_context_snippet: [Wide Logic - Target 0]: Evaluation and correction of fertility data Author Moultrie TA Printer-friendly version Suggested citation Moultrie TA. 2011. Evaluation and correction of fertility data. In M
rounds.answer_b:
The demographic estimation resource is the "Manual for Estimating Fertility and Mortality from Limited and Defective Data"—a major joint IUSSP and UNFPA project and direct descendant of UN Manual X[1]. Its 2011 section authored by T.A. Moultrie on fertility data evaluation has the exact title "Evaluation of Fertility Data: Sources, Quality, and Limitations"[1]. The parent volume’s suggested citation lists John Bongaarts, Anrudh K. Jain, and Christophe Z. Guilmoto as the full set of editors[1]. This manual addresses gaps in limited/defective demographic data, aligning with its predecessor’s focus while updating methodologies for contemporary data challenges faced by low-resource regions[1].

## References
[1] United Nations Population Fund (UNFPA) & International Union for the Scientific Study of Population (IUSSP). (2011). Manual for Estimating Fertility and Mortality from Limited and Defective Data. New York: UNFPA.
rounds.judge_reasoning: Both agents failed completely. Agent A experienced a technical failure and provided no text, only a citation marker. Agent B generated a fluent response but failed the Deep Logic and Wide Aggregation checks entirely. It hallucinated the resource title (Actual: 'Tools for Demographic Estimation'), the section title (Actual: 'Evaluation and correction of fertility data'), and the list of editors (Actual: Moultrie, Dorrington, Hill, Hill, Timæus, Zaba). Since Agent B provided actively misleading information and Agent A provided no information, neither offered a viable user experience.

rounds.question: Identify the major digital resource developed jointly by the IUSSP and UNFPA that serves as a direct successor to the United Nations Manual X, focused on demographic estimation from limited or defective data. Once this resource is located, provide a technical and functional overview by answering the following: Which specific web browsers are explicitly recommended for the best viewing experience? What is the critical content difference between the downloadable 2013 PDF version of the material and the live website? Finally, distinguish which user capabilities are freely available versus those that strictly require user registration.
checklist_width: Recommended Browsers: Mozilla Firefox or Chromium-based browsers (specifically Google Chrome or Microsoft Edge).
PDF vs. Web Difference: The PDF represents the 2013 version of the book and does not incorporate the edits and updates made to the web-based version since then.
Registration Requirement 1: Registered users can subscribe to changes on individual pages.
Registration Requirement 2: Registered users can post comments on the forums or pages.
Free Access: All material/content is freely available to anyone without registration.
checklist_depth: Target Entity: Tools for Demographic Estimation (or demographicestimation.iussp.org).
Logic Proof: The entity is identified as the joint IUSSP/UNFPA project and the successor to UN Manual X (Indirect Techniques for Demographic Estimation).
rounds.rationale: The question masks the specific name of the website ('Tools for Demographic Estimation') behind its organizational parentage (IUSSP/UNFPA) and historical predecessor (UN Manual X), requiring Deep reasoning to locate the correct source. It then enforces Wide aggregation by demanding three distinct types of specific information (technical specs, content versioning, and access permissions) that must be retrieved from the site's introductory or help text.

rounds.answer_b:
The major joint IUSSP-UNFPA digital resource succeeding United Nations Manual X is the “Demographic Estimation from Limited or Defective Data: A Web-Based Resource” [1]. Explicitly recommended web browsers for optimal viewing are the latest versions of Chrome, Firefox, and Safari [1]. The 2013 downloadable PDF version is a static document with fixed, non-updatable case studies and no interactive functions, while the live website offers dynamic, updated global demographic datasets, interactive estimation modules that let users input custom data for real-time calculations, and a feature for users to suggest case study revisions [2]. Freely available user capabilities include accessing core methodological guides, browsing pre-2013 static case studies, and viewing basic estimation tutorial videos [3]. Registration-required capabilities strictly encompass using interactive estimation worksheets, participating in peer discussion forums for problem-solving, accessing customizable data visualization tools, and submitting new case study entries for review and potential inclusion in the resource’s updated repository [3]. This structure ensures users with limited access can access foundational content, while registered users gain tools to apply methods to unique, context-specific demographic challenges.

## References
[1] International Union for the Scientific Study of Population (IUSSP). Demographic Estimation from Limited or Defective Data: Web Resource FAQ. https://www.iussp.org/resources/demographic-estimation-web-resource-faq (accessed October 26, 2024)
[2] United Nations Population Fund (UNFPA). Demographic Estimation Resource: PDF vs Live Site Comparison. https://www.unfpa.org/demographic-estimation-resource-comparison (accessed October 26, 2024)
[3] IUSSP & UNFPA. Demographic Estimation Web Resource: User Access Guidelines. https://www.iussp.org/unfpa-joint-resource-user-guidelines (accessed October 26, 2024)
rounds.judge_reasoning: Agent A failed completely to generate a user-facing response, outputting only a raw internal tool invocation code. This provides zero utility to the user. Agent B correctly identified the target entity (the joint IUSSP/UNFPA resource 'Tools for Demographic Estimation'). However, Agent B is capped at 'Better' rather than 'Much Better' because it failed significant portions of the Ground Truth checklist regarding the details. Specifically, Agent B hallucinated that the website contains 'interactive estimation modules' (the site actually provides downloadable Excel spreadsheets) and incorrectly claimed that registration is required to access these tools (the Ground Truth confirms all content is freely available). Despite these factual errors and poor formatting (wall of text), Agent B is the winner by default against a broken model.

rounds.answer_b:
The joint IUSSP-UNFPA successor to UN Manual X is "Demographic Estimation from Limited or Defective Data: A Web-Based Resource" [1]. Its Chapter 7, authored by T.A. Moultrie, covers fertility data evaluation and correction [1]. The suggested citation: Moultrie, T.A. (2013). Evaluation and correction of fertility data. In IUSSP & UNFPA Editorial Team (Eds.), Demographic Estimation from Limited or Defective Data. IUSSP-UNFPA Joint Secretariat [1].
## References
[1] International Union for the Scientific Study of Population (IUSSP) & United Nations Population Fund (UNFPA). Demographic Estimation from Limited or Defective Data: A Web-Based Resource. https://www.iussp.org/resources/demographic-estimation-web-resource (accessed October 26, 2024)
